chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P No.D-114 of 2026 [Mehar Abbas v. Province of Sindh and 04 others] DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on M.A No.481/2026 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection (s) 3. For orders on M.A No.482/2026 (Exemption) 4. For hearing of main case 27.01.2026 Mr.Ashar Majeed Khokhar, Advocate for the Petitioner ******* 1. Granted. 2to4. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that pursuant to Consolidated Advertisement No.06/2024, issued by the Sindh Public Service Commission (SPSC) inviting online applications for various posts in different departments, including the post of Assistant Sub‐Inspector (BPS‐09), the petitioner applied online for the said post. He further submits that the petitioner thereafter appeared in the physical test conducted by the respondent- Commission and was declared successful. It is contended that the petitioner subsequently appeared in the written test, in which he was also declared successful and thereafter appeared in the viva‐voce/interview; however, his result has not been declared neither as passed nor failed. Learned counsel submits that the petitioner moved a formal application/representation to respondent No.3 Chairman SPSC, Hyderabad (available at page-73 of the Court file), but no response was furnished. He further submits that on 13.01.2026, the petitioner again submitted a representation to respondent No.3 under Regulation 161 of the Sindh Public Service Commission (Recruitment Management) Regulations, 2023 (available at page-77 of the Court file), which also remains undecided. When confronted with the fact that the petitioner has already availed the statutory remedy, learned counsel states that he would be satisfied if directions are issued to respondent No.3 to decide

He further submits that on 13.01.2026, the petitioner again submitted a representation to respondent No.3 under Regulation 161 of the Sindh Public Service Commission (Recruitment Management) Regulations, 2023 (available at page-77 of the Court file), which also remains undecided. When confronted with the fact that the petitioner has already availed the statutory remedy, learned counsel states that he would be satisfied if directions are issued to respondent No.3 to decide C.P No.D-114 of 2026 2 of 2 the pending representation expeditiously in accordance with law and he seeks disposal of the petition on that basis. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, learned Additional Advocate General Sindh, present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice of the petition. A copy of the memo of petition has been supplied to him. After examining the same, he assures if the petitioner’s representation is pending, it shall be decided strictly in accordance with law. Accordingly, the instant petition stands disposed of along with pending application (s), with directions to the Secretary, SPSC, to seek nomination of a Member to hear the petitioner’s representation (available at page 77 of the Court file) as required under Regulation 161 ibid. The nominated Member shall conduct a hearing, evaluate the material and pass a speaking order after providing a fair opportunity of hearing to the petitioner, within thirty days from the date of receipt of this order. Let a copy of this order be communicated to respondent No.3 (Chairman, Sindh Public Service Commission, Hyderabad) and to the learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh, for compliance and information. JUDGE JUDGE AHSAN K. ABRO
